title: NGO Darpan Registration

hero.headline: Get Your NGO DARPAN ID with Expert Assistance
hero.subtext: Trusted by NGOs across India. Fast processing, government-compliant, 100% accurate.

mainHeading: NGO Darpan Registration Online – NITI Aayog NGO Registration in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: NGO Darpan is an initiative of NITI Aayog and National Informatics Centre (NIC) that enables NGOs and Voluntary Organizations (VOs) across India to associate with the Government of India (GoI). The objective of this portal is to make all information and resources of the Government of India available to the general public, to enhance transparency, efficiency, and alignment with the civil society and to implement projects in a timely and efficient manner. Any NGO seeking government grants or seeking to work with different ministries and departments are required to register on NGO Darpan. It provides a unique Darpan ID which is necessary for grant applications, and official correspondence. Professionals Law provides end-to-end support for NGO Darpan registration, enabling you to formulate accurate documentation, comply with regulations, and connect effortlessly with government departments. We ensure your NGO is rightly Registered and avail Central Schemes and Grants.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: The registration is compulsory for NGOs to get financial help and to work with the central government ministries. It confirms the NGO’s existence as well as credibility and makes them available to apply schemes, CSR money, and grants. Registration: This promotes transparency in operation, and trust for the funders. Professionals Law assists NGOs in following the process, become compliant, and get the valid Darpan ID. Once Professionals Law supports your NGO, your NGO can apply to so many schemes and funding from different ministries and departments.

criteria: Any NGO registered as a Society, Trust, or Section 8 Company under relevant Indian laws
Must have a valid registration certificate (PAN is mandatory)
Must be operational and actively working in the area of social welfare, development, or public service
Must have details of governing body and activities undertaken

body.title: Access to Government Grants:
body.description: Registered NGOs can apply for grants and schemes offered by various ministries and government departments.

body.title: Enhanced Credibility:
body.description: Registration establishes the NGO’s credibility and transparency, which is vital for securing public or private funding.

body.title: Mandatory for CSR Funding:
body.description: Many corporates mandate NGO Darpan registration to disburse CSR funds as per Companies Act, 2013.

body.title: Unique Darpan ID:
body.description: A unique ID issued helps NGOs maintain a consistent profile for various compliance and funding purposes.

body.title: Ease of Collaboration:
body.description: Darpan registration streamlines interactions with NITI Aayog and simplifies future engagements with governmental bodies.

documents: PAN & Aadhaar of at least 3 governing members
Memorandum of Association / Trust Deed / MOA
Annual Report or Activities Report (preferably of last 1 to 2 years)
Copy of PAN and Aadhaar of office bearers
Details of key personnel with contact and address
Email ID and Mobile Number of the NGO

body.description: Professionals Law collects necessary documents and information from the NGO.
A profile is created on the NGO Darpan portal.
All relevant documents and member details are submitted.
The application is reviewed, and a Darpan ID is issued by NITI Aayog.
Professionals Law assists in linking the Darpan ID with relevant ministry or department for grant application.
Professionals Law helps maintain and update NGO Darpan profile annually or as required.

body.title: Linking with Ministries
body.description: 7 to 10 days depending on ministry protocols

body.title: Professionals Law ensures smooth and timely execution
body.description: within 7 to 10 business days on average.

body.title: Suspension or blacklisting of Darpan ID
Ineligibility for government schemes or CSR funds
Legal action under applicable laws for falsified records

body.question: Is NGO Darpan registration mandatory?
body.answer: It is mandatory for NGOs seeking government grants or CSR funding.

body.question: Who manages the NGO Darpan portal?
body.answer: The portal is maintained by NITI Aayog and the National Informatics Centre (NIC).

body.question: Can a new NGO apply for Darpan ID?
body.answer: Yes, but having at least one year of operational history is beneficial.

body.question: Is it necessary to be a registered NGO to apply?
body.answer: Yes, only registered Societies, Trusts, or Section 8 Companies can apply.

body.question: How long does it take to get a Darpan ID?
body.answer: Generally, it takes 5–7 days post successful submission and verification.

body.question: Does Professionals Law help in updating existing NGO Darpan profiles?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides services for editing, updating, or revalidating Darpan IDs.

body.question: Is renewal required after getting the Darpan ID?
body.answer: No formal renewal, but NGOs must update the profile regularly, especially before applying for grants.

body.question: Can we apply for multiple grants with one Darpan ID?
body.answer: Yes, a single Darpan ID can be used for applying across ministries.

body.question: What if the application is rejected?
body.answer: Professionals Law assists in reviewing, correcting, and reapplying for Darpan ID if initially rejected.
